entry.title: Java + RocksDB - JNI
entry.summary: Building an experimental RocksDB wrapper for Java using the FFM API instead of JNI: how MemorySegment and Arena work, design choices behind the type-safe API, and lessons from building native bindings without a JNI glue layer.

entry.title: Coding with Claude Code: A Week on hosh
entry.summary: A week of pairing with Claude Code on a real Java project: what worked, what didn't, and why writing a CLAUDE.md is the highest-leverage first step before delegating anything to an AI agent.

entry.title: Your Compiler is already part of your Security Team
entry.summary: Domain primitives — types that encode validation into construction — eliminate entire vulnerability categories, make codebases explorable, and turn security from a checklist into a property of the design.

entry.title: Write Down the Why
entry.summary: How writing down the reasoning behind team decisions — testing philosophy, branching model — creates guidance that survives team turnover, scales better than procedures alone, and now serves as the prompt for AI agents too.

entry.title: The Joy of Proper Encapsulation
entry.summary: A wrong fix for a JPMS encapsulation warning reveals what the module system is actually telling you: the story of signal handling in hosh and why visible technical debt beats hidden dependencies.
